headline: Three labs converge on a timing gap; pickleball data gets weird and wonderful
narrative:
Your labs ran hot today. The pickle-idea-lab alone churned out 37 findings, and something interesting happened: both it and general-purpose independently flagged the same missing piece — temporal metadata. Nobody's capturing when things happen, what season they happen in, how fresh the data is. That's a real gap, and two separate teams noticed it without talking to each other. That's signal.

Pickleball is getting serious. Your labs found four entire equipment categories where zero brands are showing up — that's sponsorship whitespace you could actually sell to manufacturers. There's also a pattern emerging: overhead shots go viral at 5x the rate of other clips. Coaching is another angle — a 'bootcamp' built on rapid video bounties could work. The data teams are also getting smarter; they found five badge types the model keeps missing and figured out why (need better positive examples in the training).

General-purpose discovered something weirder: an 'unsexy brilliance' index that might explain why certain things succeed quietly instead of going viral. They also noticed the dataset is heavily dominated by low-quality amateur footage (26% of everything) — which might be a feature, not a bug, depending on what you're trying to learn. Meta-lab is now proposing a new lab to dig into authenticity and production quality, because that's becoming the thread connecting everything.

Bottom line: your system is finding convergent signals, which means it's working. You've got gaps to fill, opportunities to chase, and one new lab asking to exist.

callouts.title: Temporal Metadata Gap (Converged Signal)
callouts.why_matters: Two independent labs flagged this same missing data — that's your system telling you something structural is broken. You're flying blind on seasonality and recency.
callouts.action: Decide: do you patch existing labs to capture date/season info, or does this become core to whatever you build next?

callouts.title: New Lab Proposal: Authenticity & Production Quality
callouts.why_matters: Your meta-lab thinks this deserves its own research team. It connects to the amateur footage patterns and could explain viral vs non-viral behavior.
callouts.action: Review the proposal. If it makes sense, spin it up. If not, tell meta-lab why.

callouts.title: Pickleball Sponsorship Whitespace
callouts.why_matters: Four equipment categories have zero brand presence. That's either an oversight or an opportunity to own something.
callouts.action: Flag this for your business team — is this worth pursuing or noise?

callouts.title: Badge Model Needs Tuning
callouts.why_matters: Five badge types are consistently missed (third shot drop artist, hustler). Pickle-lab figured out why: positive examples missing. Quick win.
callouts.action: Add positive examples to the prompt. Rerun. Watch F1 scores jump.

callouts.title: Overhead Shot Viral Pattern
callouts.why_matters: One specific shot type outperforms others by 5x. Could be content direction for coaching bootcamp idea.
callouts.action: Just notice this. Might inform product or content strategy.

pickle-idea-lab.top_title: Equipment Durability Intelligence — Sell to Manufacturers
